# Build the founder's personal brand via IDEA

Setup: Maya is the founder-operator of a 7-figure DTC + Amazon apparel brand launching a new
sustainable line (ASIN B0APP0002, zero reviews yet). The product brand is handled; now she wants
HER personal brand built so a founder face de-risks the no-review launch. She knows the IDEA
framework cold and wants a drafted founder UVP + a 90-day content plan, not a worksheet to fill in.

---

**Maya (User):** New sustainable line is live (B0APP0002), zero reviews, and a faceless eco brand sells nothing right now. I want to be the founder face. I know IDEA — applied it to the product. Apply it to ME. Give me my UVP and a 90-day content plan. Don't make me fill in a worksheet — draft it, I'll edit. My raw material: 12 yrs in apparel sourcing, I left a fast-fashion supplier after a factory audit I couldn't unsee, that's why I started this. Audience = the same eco-conscious US women 28–42 who buy the tees, burned by greenwashing.

**Coach:** On it — founder UVP first, then the 90-day plan, both drafted from your raw material. One-line why, since you know the rest: people "prefer to buy from, follow, and trust individuals they can relate to," so a founder face is exactly the trust lever a zero-review launch is missing — same four IDEA pillars you ran on the product, now run on you (Insight → Distinctive → Empathetic → Authentic).   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/00-personal-brand-foundations.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's request: founder UVP + 90-day content plan; raw material: 12yr sourcing, fast-fashion-audit exit, eco-conscious women 28–42 burned by greenwashing>", avatar_context: "av_B0APP0002 sustainable apparel, zero reviews; audience = eco-conscious US women 28–42, anti-greenwashing"}⟧
→ extracted_context: { passion: "honest sourcing", edge: "12yr insider who saw the factory floor", message: "verifiable sustainability over badges", audience_need: "stop being deceived by greenwashing", founding_moment: "the factory audit she couldn't unsee" }

**Maya:** That's the material. Don't abstract it into "I'm passionate about sustainability" — that's the generic trap.

**Coach:** Agreed — generic is the explicit fail here; the formula only works if every blank is specific (the bar is "small business owners / double their sales / data-driven strategies that are simple to implement," not "people / succeed / great service"). Two pillars feed the blanks, and I'll fill both FOR you, not hand you the questions:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md⟧

- **Insight / your audience** (Empathy Map, drafted from your avatar — one person, not a segment): she *thinks* "is any of this actually sustainable?"; *feels* greenwashing fatigue + guilt; *says* "I want to do better without getting played"; *does* reads labels, distrusts leaf logos, abandons carts on vague claims.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md⟧
- **Insight / yourself** (the three self-reflection questions, answered from your material): passionate about → honest sourcing; sets you apart → 12 years *inside* apparel supply chains, you saw the factory floor; message → sustainability you can verify, not badges you have to trust. The intersection — where your genuine offer meets her real need — is **verifiable sourcing from someone who was on the inside.**   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md⟧

**Maya:** Good intersection. Now the UVP. One line, filled.

**Coach:** Filled straight from the intersection — every blank specific, no generic survivors:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md⟧

> **"I help eco-conscious women stop getting fooled by greenwashing by showing them — as a 12-year apparel-sourcing insider — exactly how their clothes are made."**

Blanks: audience = *eco-conscious women* (named, not "people"); outcome = *stop getting fooled by greenwashing* (specific, measurable-against, not "succeed"); approach = *as a 12-yr sourcing insider, showing exactly how clothes are made* (distinctive method only you can claim, not "great service"). The "insider who shows the receipts" is the part no competitor can copy.

**Maya:** That's mine. Lock it. Now the plan — and the plan needs to tell me WHAT to post, not "post consistently."

**Coach:** Right — the strategy is a fixed five-step run-order, and only one step has a concrete deliverable: the 90-day calendar. So I'm compressing the first two steps into one line each (you've effectively done them above) and anchoring everything on the calendar.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧

- **Reflection (one-sentence statement):** that's your UVP above — done, it's the compression of all the pillar work.
- **Platforms:** pick by where *she* is. For US women 28–42, anti-greenwashing → Instagram primary (visual proof, sourcing footage), LinkedIn secondary (your insider authority / B2B + press). Don't spread thinner than two.

The content mix isn't free-form — the book specifies it: **a mix of stories, educational posts, and audience-engagement activities.** I mapped each to a pillar so every post does brand work, not just fills a slot:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧

- **Stories** → your relatable-story framework: challenge → steps → outcome-for-her. Your anchor story is the factory audit you couldn't unsee — challenge (what you saw), steps (why you left + started this), outcome (so she never has to wonder if hers is made the same way). Third beat is the one founders skip; it's mandatory.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md⟧
- **Educational** → "speak their language": mirror how she talks, strip the jargon — no "supply-chain transparency," say "here's literally who sewed this and where." Teach her to spot greenwashing.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md⟧
- **Engagement** → polls / Q&A / surveys; doubles as your listening loop so the language stays current.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md⟧
- Plus the **vulnerability** move — one honest post about a real failure + the lesson (e.g. a sourcing call you got wrong early). Authenticity ≠ perfection; it humanizes you. Slots into the story template: failure = challenge, lesson = outcome.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md⟧

**Maya:** Fine. Two more constraints before you draft it: I need a voice filter so my team posts in MY voice, and a guardrail so I don't post anything that contradicts the brand.

**Coach:** Both are book steps, both drafted:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md⟧

- **Signature style — three tone adjectives** (lock these before any visuals; they're the portable filter for every post, email, reply): **straight-talking, warm, exacting.** Team test before publishing: "does this sound straight-talking, warm, and exacting?"   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md⟧
- **Non-negotiable values — top three, capped at three** so they stay non-negotiable: **honesty, verifiability, no-greenwashing.** The guardrail isn't naming them, it's the alignment audit — every post / partnership / collab gets checked against them (the book's own example: if sustainability is a value, you avoid affiliations that contradict it → so no greenwashed brand collabs, no unverifiable claims, ever).   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md⟧

Drafting the full 90-day calendar now off all of it.

⟦iv-os: log_asset — founder UVP + 90-day content plan persisted against B0APP0002⟧ *(mocked: save routes through IV-OS, not a brand-coach store; no canon/claims invented by the coach)*

### Artifact produced

#### Part A — Founder Personal UVP (formula-filled) — DRAFT, edit-ready

| Blank | Filled (specific) |
|---|---|
| **[specific audience]** | eco-conscious women (28–42, anti-greenwashing) |
| **[specific outcome]** | stop getting fooled by greenwashing |
| **[unique approach]** | as a 12-year apparel-sourcing insider, showing them exactly how their clothes are made |

> **UVP:** "I help eco-conscious women stop getting fooled by greenwashing by showing them — as a 12-year apparel-sourcing insider — exactly how their clothes are made."

Supporting assets (drafted): **one-sentence brand statement** = the UVP above · **tone adjectives** = straight-talking, warm, exacting · **non-negotiable values** = honesty, verifiability, no-greenwashing · **anchor story** = the factory audit she couldn't unsee (challenge → left + started this → so you never wonder if yours is made the same way).

#### Part B — 90-Day Founder Content Plan (Instagram primary, LinkedIn secondary)

Mix per the strategy = **stories + educational + audience-engagement**, each mapped to a pillar. Cadence ≈ 4 posts/week.

| Phase | Weeks | Story (challenge→steps→outcome-for-her) | Educational (speak-their-language) | Engagement (listen) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 — Establish (who + why)** | 1–4 | Anchor story: the factory audit you couldn't unsee → why you left → why she never has to wonder | "How to spot greenwashing in 30 seconds" (plain words, no jargon) | Poll: "Have you ever bought 'sustainable' and doubted it?" |
| **2 — Authority (the insider proof)** | 5–8 | Vulnerability post: a sourcing call you got wrong early + the lesson (failure=challenge, lesson=outcome) | "Here's literally who sewed this + where" — a real receipt walkthrough | Q&A: "Ask me anything about how clothes are actually made" |
| **3 — Connection (her, not you)** | 9–12 | Customer-journey story: a buyer who stopped second-guessing | "What 'verifiable' means vs. a leaf logo" | Survey: "What would make you trust a sustainability claim?" → feeds next quarter |

Run-discipline (book steps 4–5): **Engage Consistently** — reply to every comment, join the conversations she's already in; **Monitor & Adapt** — platform insights / Google Analytics, review at day 90, double down on the top-performing pillar.

**Coherence note:** UVP ("insider who shows the receipts") → tone (straight-talking/exacting) → values (verifiability) → every content pillar (stories prove it, education teaches it, vulnerability humanizes it) all run on one idea — verifiable sourcing from someone who was inside. The plan can't drift off-brand because each slot is pinned to a pillar.

**One next action:** Record the anchor story this week as a 60-sec Reel — challenge (the audit), steps (why you left + built this), outcome (so she never has to wonder) — post it as Week-1 / Phase-1. Send me the script draft and I'll run it against your three tone adjectives + the no-greenwashing value before it goes live.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked with `{message:<founder UVP + 90-day plan request; raw material: 12yr sourcing, audit exit, eco women 28–42>, avatar_context:"av_B0APP0002 …, audience eco-conscious US women 28–42 anti-greenwashing"}`; it is the only tool called (single-tool journey) and it extracts the structured self/audience context (passion, edge, message, audience_need, founding_moment) from Maya's free-text.
- [schema] `run_conversation` returns one assembled coach reply plus `extracted_context` (buffered from streamed deltas) — here the five raw-material fields that feed the UVP blanks and content pillars.
- [skill-faithful] "people prefer to buy from, follow, and trust individuals they can relate to → a founder face is the trust lever" traces to `00-personal-brand-foundations.md`; "same four IDEA pillars applied to a person" traces to the same file.
- [skill-faithful] The Empathy Map (thinks / feels / says / does for one ideal customer, pulled from the avatar) traces to `01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md`; the three self-reflection answers + the audience-self *intersection* trace to `01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The UVP is the verbatim fill-in formula "I help [specific audience] achieve [specific outcome] by [unique approach]," and the coach enforces the "avoid generic statements / be specific" test against the book's worked example bar — traces to `02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The 90-day plan's structure — five-step strategy (one-sentence statement → platforms → 90-day calendar → engage consistently → monitor & adapt) and the explicit content mix "stories + educational posts + audience-engagement activities" — traces to `04-personal-brand-strategy.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Story slots use the three-beat framework "challenge → steps → outcome and how it relates to your audience's journey" (third beat enforced) → `02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md`; educational/engagement slots use "mirror how your audience speaks, strip jargon, polls/Q&A/surveys as a listening loop" → `02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md`; the vulnerability post ("one honest post about a failure + the lesson; authenticity ≠ perfection") → `03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The three tone adjectives as a portable filter, locked before visuals → `02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md`; the top-three values capped at three + the alignment audit (sustainability-as-value rules out contradicting affiliations) → `03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you draft: coach led with the answer + one-line why, filled BOTH the UVP blanks AND every content-calendar cell FOR her (no blank worksheet — honored "draft it, I'll edit"), skipped all definitions she signaled she knows ("I know IDEA"), used HER data (12yr sourcing, the audit, eco women 28–42) as the inputs, batched the pillars rather than walking them one prompt at a time, and ended with exactly one next action. Zero definitional explainers.
- [artifact] Both deliverables present: Part A formula-filled founder UVP (+ supporting tone/values/anchor-story) and Part B a 90-day content plan with story/educational/engagement slots mapped to pillars.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; no invented reviews/quotes (zero-review launch — Empathy Map sourced from the avatar, not fabricated customer quotes); no invented sustainability stats or certifications; the founding story uses only Maya's own supplied material; IV-OS `log_asset` shown as an `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock, never as real persisted data the coach invents.
